summary: Phase 4 — School-Admin auto-attach bug-fix. Backend pytest 15/15 GREEN covering: instructor create auto-attaches school_name/school_id/school_code (Ridgeview Academy / sch_*/RVA-001), student visible in BOTH admin and instructor tables, spoof protection on POST + PUT (Hogwarts payload ignored), cross-school 403 on UPDATE and DELETE, admin manual create unchanged, bulk-create auto-attach (incl. spoof in bulk row), auto-attach also working for quiz-results & results (the other instructor-writable school-scoped kinds). Frontend partially verified: instructor add-student form has 0 school fields, and admin /admin/students table renders the SCHOOL ID + SCHOOL columns auto-populated (visible 'RVA-001 | Ridgeview Academy' on the seeded Noah Patel row). Full end-to-end UI create flow could not be completed because the /login/admin page is a card-selector (two cards: School Admin Login / Student Login) that requires an extra navigation step my Playwright script under-handled — but the API path which the form uses is independently fully covered by the backend test suite.

minor.issue: Review-request spec says: 'For other school-scoped kinds (classes, chapters, subjects, quizzes), the same auto-attach happens for instructor.' BUT KIND_PERMS (server.py L909-925) grants instructor 'write' only on students/quiz-results/results. So instructor POSTs to /api/resources/classes, /subjects, /chapters, /quizzes return 403 'Forbidden'. The auto-attach code block (server.py L1062) lists these kinds in its tuple, but _can() denies write first. Either: (a) widen KIND_PERMS so instructor can write these kinds (matches the spec), or (b) update the spec to make these admin-only and remove them from the auto-attach tuple. Not blocking the headline bug fix; flagging as a requirements/perm-table mismatch.

action_items: Decide on the KIND_PERMS gap: either grant instructor write on classes/subjects/chapters/quizzes (matches the explicit review-request bullet 'For other school-scoped kinds...'), or remove those kinds from the auto-attach scope tuple in create_resource/update_resource/bulk_create. Currently instructor gets 403 on those kinds.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py create_resource (L1058-1070): clean override pattern — overwrites school_name/school_id/school_code AFTER doc dict is built, so any client-side spoofing is reliably ignored. Correct.
server.py update_resource (L1137-1149): instructor cannot change school_name AND cannot touch records belonging to other schools (403). Two-layer defense — good.
server.py delete_resource (L1164-1168): instructor cross-school delete blocked at the same layer — consistent.
server.py bulk_create (L1207-1230): resolves school context ONCE per call (not per row) — efficient. Auto-attach applies row-by-row including spoofed rows.

KIND_PERMS table (server.py L909-925) is the single source of truth for who can write what — but it diverges from the review-request expectation for classes/subjects/chapters/quizzes (see backend_issues.minor).

frontend InstructorStudents.jsx — fields[] correctly has NO school_name entry; basePath='/resources' routes to the shared endpoint that auto-attaches. Verified: locator '[data-testid="field-school_name-input"], [data-testid="field-school_name-select"], label:has-text("School")' returns 0 on instructor add form.

frontend ManageStudents.jsx L38-42: 'School ID' column prefers r.school_code (persisted by backend) and only falls back to a schools-collection lookup if school_code is absent. Correct precedence — verified visually: admin table shows 'RVA-001' on the seeded Ridgeview student without any extra lookup latency.

success_rate.frontend: partial — instructor form (no school field) + admin table (auto-populated School/School ID columns) verified visually; full UI create-flow not completed due to login-card selector navigation in script, but the underlying API is fully covered by the backend suite

test_credentials: Admin admin@cmsedu.ai / Demo@123 ; School Admin (instructor) instructor@cmsedu.ai / Demo@123 — both work.
seed_data_creation: 13 test students + 2 'OtherSchool_*' schools were created via admin/instructor tokens during the run. Module-scoped cleanup fixture deletes them at teardown using admin token; if any persist (network flake), they have student_id prefixes STU-TST-, STU-VIS-, STU-INST-, STU-SPF-, STU-UPD-, STU-OS-, STU-OSD-, STU-ADM-, STU-BA-, STU-BB- — safe to bulk-delete by these prefixes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase-4 auto-attach bug fix is fully GREEN on the backend. The only remaining ambiguity is the KIND_PERMS / spec mismatch for classes/subjects/chapters/quizzes (instructor write currently 403'd). If the main agent widens KIND_PERMS to include instructor write on those kinds, the test_instructor_write_blocked_for_admin_only_kinds parametrized cases will need to be flipped to assert 200 with school_name=Ridgeview Academy (mirrors the existing test_auto_attach_for_writable_school_scoped_kinds pattern). For UI: /login/admin is a card selector page — click the 'School Admin Login' card first (a Sign in arrow), then the actual email/password form appears; same for Student. Use that 2-step navigation in any future Playwright UI tests for this app.
rca_of_the_issue: Original bug: School Admin POST /api/resources/students wasn't propagating school_name/school_id/school_code, so created students were invisible to both the instructor's scoped list (filtered by school_name) and the admin's table (School column empty). FIX (verified): create_resource/update_resource/bulk_create now, when user.role=='instructor' AND kind is in the school-scoped set, force-write school_name from user.school_name and look up school_id/school_code from db.schools — overriding any client payload. Update + delete additionally verify the existing record's school_name matches user.school_name before allowing the mutation (returns 403 otherwise). Frontend InstructorStudents.jsx already omitted the school_name field, so there's no UI surface for the spoof. End-to-end verified with 15 pytest cases and visual confirmation of the admin students table showing 'RVA-001 | Ridgeview Academy' on the auto-attached row.
